action.hypothesis: After 30 non-improving rounds and two failed non-architecture probes on the current `K=32` anchor, the next policy-compliant move is to test a fundamentally different existing family at another frontier-relevant small budget. `lowrank_gated_residual` is the strongest non-codebook prior at larger K, so probing it at `K=64, EF=16` under the modern pinned signum recipe tests whether a simpler gated sparse mechanism can recover a non-dominated mid-K tradeoff where the current bucketed soft-codebook backbone may be over-specialized.
action.summary: Run an architecture-only comparison at `K=64, EF=16` by switching from `bucketed_lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` to `lowrank_gated_residual` while keeping optimizer, LR, aux loss, and preprocessing fixed. This satisfies the severe-stagnation requirement to stop minor tweaks and try a fundamentally different architecture, while targeting a strategically useful smaller-K frontier slot rather than spending another round on the already-stressed `K=32` lane.
action.self_review: This round changes only architecture relative to the pinned modern recipe, so attribution stays clean. I chose `K=64` instead of `K=32` because repeated `K=32` probes have been unproductive and `lowrank_gated_residual` historically improves as K rises, making `K=64` the better chance for a new non-dominated point under the enforced `EF=16` regime.
notes_to_memory: Round 171 returns to an architecture-only move because severe stagnation policy now explicitly requires a fundamentally different architecture after failed preprocessing and loss probes.

`lowrank_gated_residual` at `K=64, EF=16` is the selected probe because gating is materially different from the current codebook backbone and historically strengthens at higher K more than at `K=24/32`.
next_hypotheses: If `lowrank_gated_residual` is competitive at `K=64, EF=16`, follow with `K=96` on the same family to map whether the gated residual lane can replace the current high-quality anchor family under the fixed EF=16 constraint.

If it is clearly worse but trains smoothly, keep the family in reserve for a later optimizer probe with Adam plus a deliberately chosen LR retune rather than discarding the optimizer-family pairing from a signum-only result.
If it fails badly, continue severe-stagnation architecture search with another fundamentally different existing family rather than returning to more minor recipe changes.

This satisfies the severe-stagnation requirement to stop minor tweaks and try a fundamentally different architecture, while targeting a strategically useful smaller-K frontier slot rather than spending another round on the already-stressed `K=32` lane.

I chose `K=64` instead of `K=32` because repeated `K=32` probes have been unproductive and `lowrank_gated_residual` historically improves as K rises, making `K=64` the better chance for a new non-dominated point under the enforced `EF=16` regime.
